title: European offer to delay sanctions on Iran still 'on the table'
description: Britain, France, and Germany said their offer to extend the Iran nuclear deal and delay sanctions for 30 days “remains on the table”, said UK Ambassador Barbara Woodward at the UN Friday. The three nations triggered on Thursday the “snapback” mechanism to restore UN sanctions on Tehran for violating its 2015 nuclear commitments.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: Kenya exhumes 32 bodies as outcry grows over starvation cult deaths
description:
In tonight's edition, 32 more bodies unearthed in Kenya reportedly linked to a doomsday cult that left over 400 followers dead two years ago.
 
Also, Human Rights Watch accuses Mauritanian security forces of committing abuses against migrants, including torture and rape, in a new report.
 
And a new wave of comedians in Burundi take on the government and the tightly controlled media with punchlines.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: Macron vows to serve out mandate to its end despite budget crisis
description: French President Emmanuel Macron said he would continue his mandate until the end of its term in 2027, despite a high-stakes confidence vote on September 8 that could bring down the government and plunge France into a new period of prolonged instability.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: Gaza famine, Trump's Wild West, France's implosion, Russia bombs Kyiv
description:
In this edition of The World This Week: 
 
The UN has this week, for the first time, declared a state of famine in Gaza. The organisation says it's "engineered" and "manmade".
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: Trump withdraws Secret Service protection for Kamala Harris
description: US President Donald Trump has terminated Secret Service protection for Kamala Harris, the former vice president and his rival in the 2024 election, officials said Friday. The move comes as Harris prepares to go on a book tour this fall to promote her account of her short presidential run against Trump.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: Corruption scandal threatens Argentina’s right-wing President Milei and his influential sister
description: Protesters pelted Argentina’s President Javier Milei with stones on Wednesday after audio recordings leaked by the press suggest that Milei and his influential sister Karina were involved in embezzling public funds from the National Disability Agency. President Milei denies the accusations.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: Macron says France and Germany are determined to protect EU digital legislation
description: French President Emmanuel Macron said on August 29 that France and Germany were determined to preserve European legislation on digital issues, after US President Donald Trump criticised EU rules on digital services. France 24 Douglas Herbert tells us more.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: France and Germany pledge to ramp up pressure on Putin
description: The leaders of France and Germany pledged Friday to increase pressure on Russia over its ongoing war in Ukraine, warning that Russian President Vladimir Putin shows no signs of ending the conflict. Both countries meanwhile decided to send additional air defence hardware to Ukraine after Russia's massive attacks this week on Kyiv.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: Thai prime minister removed by court, triggering power scramble
description: Thailand's Constitutional Court dismissed Prime Minister Paetongtarn Shinawatra on August 29 for an ethics violation, in another crushing blow to the Shinawatra political dynasty that triggered a flurry of deal-making aimed at filling the void.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: Major Russian strike on Kyiv sparks outrage among European leaders
description: The death toll in a major Russian missile and drone strike on the Ukrainian capital rose to 23, including four children, officials said on August 29, as U.S.-led efforts to end the three-year war remain stuck in apparent limbo.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: Paul McCartney's personal photographs on display in London
description:
It’s been more than six decades since the rise of Beatlemania. Now, the discovery of some long-lost photos offers a behind-the-scenes look at the group’s early journey to superstardom.
 
Photographs taken by Paul McCartney are on display at the Gagosian Gallery in London, giving a glimpse into their beginnings in Europe, including France, before they took America by storm.
 
Monte Francis takes a closer look at the exhibit, which runs through 4 October.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: Israel says bodies of 2 hostages returned as military begins Gaza City offensive
description: Israel’s military said it suspended midday pauses to fighting, which had allowed food and aid supplies to enter from 10 a.m. to 8 p.m. in Gaza’s largest city. It also said it had recovered the bodies of two hostages and vowed its military offensive would return more. Noga Tarnopolsky tells us more.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: Trump seeks to scrap $5 billion in foreign aid
description: US President Donald Trump is seeking to claw back $5 billion in congressionally-approved funding for foreign aid programmes – a move that is likely to spur backlash from Democrats and increase the likelihood of a government shutdown.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: Is Modi snubbing Trump amid tariff spat?
description: In this edition of Access Asia, we explore how New Delhi is grappling with new 50 percent US tariffs taking effect. Will the souring relations between Narendra Modi and Donald Trump force India to rethink its ties with the United States and look further east? Meanwhile, South Korea scores another pop-culture win as its animated musical "K-Pop Demon Hunters" smashes Netflix records.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: UN sanctions on Iran: Tehran blasts 30-day ultimatum by Europe
description: France, Britain and Germany have launched the process of triggering the “snapback mechanism,” which automatically reimposes all United Nations sanctions on Iran over its nuclear program. The three countries say Iran has willfully violated the 2015 nuclear deal that had lifted those measures. Our guest, Simon Mabon, Professor of International Politics at Lancaster University, explains what this means.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: Palestinian director Scandar Copti's 'Happy Holidays': A bold portrait of life between two worlds
description: In this episode of arts24, Eve Jackson sits down with Scandar Copti – a Palestinian filmmaker, Israeli citizen and one of the most original cinematic voices working today. His new film, "Happy Holidays", is set in the northern city of Haifa and unfolds in four interwoven chapters, following the intersecting lives of Palestinian Christians and Jewish Israelis. As these characters navigate love, gender roles and the invisible weight of politics, the film reveals a society shaped as much by silence as by conflict.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: War in Ukraine: EU leaders slam Russia over Kyiv strike
description: Russia pounded Ukraine with deadly missile and drone strikes early on 28 August in a sweeping attack that the US special envoy on Ukraine said undermined President Donald Trump’s peace efforts. We discuss this with our correspondent in Berlin, Nick Holdsworth, and our guests Stefan Wolff, Professor of International Security at the University of Birmingham, and Jeanette Süß, Research Fellow at the Study Committee on Franco-German Relations.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: EU leaders vow 'support' to Moldova in face of Russia
description: On August 27, the leaders of France, Germany and Poland expressed their support for Moldova’s bid to join the EU, denouncing Russian “lies” and “hybrid attacks” during a symbolic visit to the former Soviet republic bordering Ukraine. Our guest, Laurențiu Pleșca, Program Coordinator at the German Marshall Fund, explains the significance of this show of support.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: Should we panic about plummeting birthrates?
description: In a special edition marking the start of a new season, we dive into an issue increasingly alarming governments around the world: a global decline in birthrates. A recent joint study by UNFPA and YouGov — spanning 14 countries and covering 37 percent of the world’s population — reveals a stark trend: fertility rates have dropped below 2.1 births per woman in over half of the nations surveyed. That's the critical threshold needed to maintain population levels without immigration.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines

title: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz visits France amid political uncertainty
description: The German and the French government are scheduled to hold a joint cabinet meeting in Brégançon and Toulon on August 28 and 29.
domain: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
